title: Purchasing and Acquisition
description: Conroe ISD follows specific rules when spending public money to buy goods and services, ensuring purchases are made fairly and responsibly. These guidelines help prevent waste and require the district to seek competitive pricing on larger purchases, so taxpayer dollars go as far as possible. Community members can trust that school funds are being managed with accountability and transparency.

timeline_local.summary: Revisions to Board Policy CH (Local) were discussed. No action was taken.

## Board Policy — Second Reading (Action Items)

### Items A through H (State-Mandated Updates):
Policies: CH Local, CKC Local, CK Local, CL Local, DGC Local, EIA Local, FFAC Local, FFB Local
- Board noted these are state-mandated changes from the legislative session, not district-initiated
- Discussion about future process: putting routine legal updates on consent agenda; Dr. Vinson committed to providing small group review and board brief previews
- Motion included authorization for non-substantive g
timeline_local.notes: Listed as a Board Policy Discussion Item. No action was taken. No specific change language available in these pages.

timeline_local.summary: This is a summaries page covering multiple policy changes: (1) BBE(Local): Informed by HB 4310 — requires Board member PIR requests be fulfilled promptly and at no cost; establishes new process for handling requests for potentially confidential information. (2) CH(Local): Informed by SB 12 & 1173 — permits raising aggregate purchasing threshold from $50,000 to $100,000; adds non-discrimination clause for HUBs. (3) CKC(Local): Informed by HB 121 — requires district emergency plan to include provisions for safety of students, staff, and spectators during District-sponsored/sanctioned extracurricular activities. (4) CKE(Local): Informed by HB 1458 — adds process for Board to authorize use of reserve police officers, with new section on procedures, authority, standards, and limitations. (5) CL(Local): Informed by SB 8 ('bathroom bill') — defines multiple-occupancy private spaces, requires sex-designated signage, outlines exceptions (young children, those needing assistance), requires facility use aligned with sex at birth, adds locker room as instructional classroom section, and outlines violation process.
changes_added: BBE(Local): Board member PIR requests must be fulfilled promptly and at no cost
BBE(Local): New process for handling requests for potentially confidential information
CH(Local): Aggregate purchasing threshold increase from $50,000 to $100,000 (optional for districts)
CH(Local): Non-discrimination clause for HUBs
CKC(Local): Emergency plan must include provisions for safety of students, staff, and spectators during District-sponsored or District-sanctioned extracurricular activities
CKE(Local): New policy section outlining process for Board authorization of reserve police officers, including procedures, authority, standards, and limitations
CL(Local): Definition of multiple-occupancy private spaces
CL(Local): Requirement for appropriate signage consistent with sex designated for each space
CL(Local): Specific exceptions (young children, individuals requiring assistance)
CL(Local): Requirement to use facilities aligned with sex at birth
CL(Local): New section addressing use of locker rooms as instructional classrooms
CL(Local): Process for violations of the policy
$100,000 (the '100' portion appears in red/underlined, indicating the new threshold amount)
Bids, Quotes, And Other Proposals Under $100,000 (section header updated)

Nothing in Board Policy may be construed to limit or prohibit the District from contracting with historically underutilized businesses or businesses owned by members of a minority group or by women in accordance with applicable state law; however, all vendors, including historically underutilized businesses, must meet the same qualifications, performance standards, and requirements applicable to all District vendors.
Aggregate purchasing threshold increase from $50,000 to $100,000 (CH Local)
Non-discrimination clause for HUBs while maintaining same standards for all vendors (CH Local)
New requirements for interim reports when a student's performance is unsatisfactory (EIA Local)
Parents must be offered at least two opportunities for teacher conferences (EIA Local)
Progress reports must require a parent's signature at least every 12 weeks (EIA Local)

No revisions to CH are proposed; it is cited as existing authority.

timeline_local.summary: This update to Conroe ISD's Purchasing and Acquisition policy (CH Local) raises the board-approval threshold from $50,000 to $100,000, replaces prior pre-approval requirements with quarterly cumulative reporting to the Board, and adds a new "Vendor Alignment" section requiring vendors who interact with students to acknowledge and agree to district policies—explicitly prohibiting content on topics such as gender fluidity, Critical Race Theory, DEI, and anti-American sentiments.
changes_added: $100,000 (replacing $50,000 as the purchasing authority threshold)

shall be reported to the Board in a quarterly report. The District shall provide the Board a cumulative report in December, March, June and September, detailing year-to-date expenditures by vendor that exceed $100,000. Each report shall clearly identify all new vendors for the quarter and include the associated commodity category.
Vendor Alignment section heading (new bold/underlined heading)

To ensure alignment with the District's adopted policies, educational goals, community standards, and all applicable state and federal laws, any vendor or contractor providing services through direct interaction with students—whether in person or via digital means such as online courses, video conferences, video streaming, or other virtual platforms—or through the development, delivery, or distribution of print or digital materials, shall, prior to purchase, acknowledge and agree through a process designed by the Superintendent and approved by the Board to comply with the requirements of EMB (Local). This vendor alignment process specifically prohibits the inclusion of the following topics, except to the extent that the topic is expressly required as part of the Texas Essential Knowledge and Skills (TEKS):
1. Gender fluidity, sexual orientation, gender, transitioning;
2. Pronoun usage inconsistent with biological sex;
3. Social and Emotional Learning Concepts that are divisive, conflict with District policy, and are inconsistent with the District's education goals;
4. Critical Race Theory;
5. Systemic Discrimination Ideologies;
6. Diversity, Equity, Inclusion (DEI); and
7. Unpatriotic, anti-American sentiments.

This requirement includes, but is not limited to services involving curriculum design, in-district professional development concerning direct student outcomes, athletic or professional coaching, student advisory services, specialized lessons, or similar educational or instructional support activities.

Failure to adhere to the terms of this Vendor Alignment, or any verified misalignment with the District's policies or community values as stated, may constitute grounds for immediate termination of the contract, subject to applicable contractual and legal provisions.
$100,000 (in competitive procurement threshold on page 3)
$100,000 (in 'Bids, Quotes, and Other Proposals Under $100,000' subheading on page 3)

description_local: This policy governs how the school district purchases goods and services, setting the rules for how money is spent on everything from supplies and equipment to contracts with outside vendors. It applies to district staff and administrators who make purchasing decisions on behalf of the schools. For parents and community members, this policy matters because it helps ensure taxpayer dollars are spent responsibly, fairly, and in accordance with state law.
